title: Pentastar Alignment
content:

{{Quote|The New Republic is still merely the Rebellion, thinly disguised as an official entity. They are outlaws, and they will continue to think and do as such. In the Pentastar Alignment, there will be no tolerance for any Rebel insurrection.|Grand Moff Ardus Kaine|<ref name="TPA" />}}
The '''Pentastar Alignment''', also known as the '''Pentastar Alignment of Powers''', was a corporatist [[Dictatorship/Legends|dictatorship]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], with its [[Capital/Legends|capital]] based on [[Entralla/Legends|Entralla]]. A successor of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], it was largely isolationist, with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] for the most part ignoring the Alignment. However, in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the Pentastar Alignment was annexed into the [[Imperial Remnant]], another successor [[Politics/Legends|state]] of the Old Empire.

The Pentastar Alignment was [[Pentastar Alignment Treaty|formed]] from a [[Ardus Kaine faction|splinter faction]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] led by [[Ardus Kaine]] in what was the "[[Velcar Free Commerce Zone]]" in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] after the [[Pentastar Talks]]. Seceding from the collapsing Empire, the Pentastar Alignment sought not to join the newly-formed New Republic and be left alone without interference from it.

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the Pentastar Alignment contributed some forces to [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], but did not formally join him during his [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. [[10 ABY/Legends|One year]] later, the [[Cloning/Legends|resurrected]] Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] began to reunite various Imperial splinter factions and rump states, as part of pre-[[Operation Shadow Hand]], in an effort to topple the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The Pentastar Alignment allied with Palpatine's resurrected clone. However, Kaine was killed by the Republic during the [[Battle of Palanhi (Galactic Civil War)|campaign]] over the planet [[Palanhi/Legends|Palanhi]], leading to it being weaken and finally absorbed into the Imperial Remnant.

The [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] served as a major figure within the Pentastar Alignment.

==History==
===Foundation===
{{Quote|The New Order has never fallen. Only the Emperor. … The Emperor may be dead, but the Empire lives on!|Ardus Kaine, Pentastar Talks|<ref name="TPA" />}}
[[File:ArdusKaine.jpg|thumb|left|120px|[[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]] founded the Pentastar Alignment.]]
After the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] fragmented into many pieces. Instead of trying to keep control of the complete [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]], who had earlier replaced Grand Moff [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin]] at the head of the [[Oversector Outer]], concentrated his forces in fourteen [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] of the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]].<ref name="TEA" />

Kaine used his personal [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]], the [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']], for some none-too-subtle intimidation as a venue to hold [[Pentastar Talks|talks]] with representatives of multiple star systems and five major Imperial corporations.<ref name="TPA" /> The talks were successful, and the Pentastar Alignment [[Pentastar Alignment Treaty|was founded]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="TEGTW" />

===Supporting Thrawn===
[[File:ReaperStarDestroyer.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']] leading the Pentastar forces.]]
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] returned from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] in a last-ditch [[Thrawn campaign|attempt]] to [[Thrawn's confederation|reunite]] and consolidate the faltering Empire. The Alignment contributed some forces to Thrawn's efforts but did not formally join him,<ref name="TEA" /> preferring to continue its militarization in anticipation of conflict with the New Republic.<ref name="BOG" />

===Death of Kaine===
[[10 ABY/Legends|One year]] later, the [[Transfer essence|resurrected]] [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] began to [[Imperial Coalition|reunite]] various Imperial splinter factions as part of [[Operation Shadow Hand]], an effort to topple the New Republic. The Emperor was more successful than Thrawn in recruiting Kaine, and the Pentastar Alignment forces eventually attacked the New Republic. Joining Thrawn's remnant forces, Ardus Kaine made a successful campaign against the New Republic forces, winning a dozen victories until penetrating the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. However, the conflict was a failure, with Kaine killed and Palpatine defeated for the last time.<ref name="TEA" />

===Annexation by the Imperial Remnant===
The Pentastar Alignment was annexed by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], becoming part of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. The ''Reaper'' became Pellaeon's flagship until its destruction at the [[Battle of Celanon]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]].<ref name="TEA" /> Before [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], Kaine's homeworld Sartinaynian—renamed Bastion—became the Imperial Remnant's capital, remaining thus for over a century.<ref name="Leg">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Organization==
===Government and politics===
The Pentastar Alignment was a corporatist<ref name="IWDOAE">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> [[Dictatorship/Legends|dictatorship]] that was administratively split in two: "[[Order]]" and "[[Enforcement (Pentastar Alignment)|Enforcement]]." Order comprised the [[Politorate]], [[Insurrection]], [[Judgment]], and [[Protectorate (Pentastar Alignment)|Protectorate]] branches.<ref name="TPA" />

The Alignment utilized the services of several former [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]] in its Judgment branch. They were known as the [[Great InQuestor of Judgment|Great InQuestors of Judgment]] and included the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] among their roster.<ref name="TDFSP6">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}}</ref>

===Military===
{{Main|Pentastar Patrol}}
The [[Pentastar Patrol]] was the primary military of the Alignment,<ref name="TEA" /> which would design, along with [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]], the [[Enforcer-class picket cruiser|''Enforcer''-class picket cruiser]]).<ref name="TPA" />

===Outside affairs===
The Alignment largely maintained an isolationist position, and sought to be on peaceful terms with all parties. This lead to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] reciprocating and largely ignoring the Alignment<ref name="TEA" />; although [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] such as the [[Red Moons]] continued to harry the Alignment.<ref name="BOG">{{JournalCite|8|Blaze of Glory (short story)}}</ref>

<gallery>
File:Pa_politerate.svg|Politorate branch
File:Pa_insurrection.svg|Insurrection branch
File:Pa_judgement.svg|Judgment branch
File:Pa_protectorate.svg|Protectorate
File:Pentastar_patrol.svg|[[Pentastar Patrol]]
</gallery>

==Astrography==
The Alignment was made of fourteen sectors and held the allegiance of hundreds of planets.<ref name="TEA" /> Many of the planets were part of the former [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], including the "[[Velcar Free Commerce Zone]]" (containing [[Capza]], [[Entralla/Legends|Entralla]], and [[Bextar]])<ref name="TPA" /> and the worlds [[Jaemus/Legends|Jaemus]],<ref name="IAGL">{{JournalCite|4|It's a Gambler's Life}}</ref> [[Cantras Gola]],<ref name="BOG" /> [[Gabredor III/Legends|Gabredor III]],<ref name="BOG" /> [[Bescane/Legends|Bescane]], and [[Criton's Point]].<ref name="TPA" />

[[File:PentastarAlignment-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Map of the Pentastar Alignment including the future [[Imperial Remnant]].]]

Other supporters of the Alignment included [[Dynamic Automata]],<ref name="TPA" /> [[PowerOn Conglomerate]],<ref name="BOG" /> and [[Galentro Heavy Works]].<ref name="TPA" />
